warnings: Dropped 1 non-scored row(s) worth 0 points (Penalty Points (Dress Code / Late Arrival)). Check they really aren't scored.
_review.note: Machine-parsed from the official rating sheet. plan.md F3: nobody may be graded against this until a human confirms it.
_review.paraphrase_note: Descriptions and level descriptors are restated in our own words (plan.md §20). Criterion names and every point value are exactly as published on the official rating sheet.

criteria.name: Identifies and defines ethical issues presented in the topic
criteria.description: Clearly recognizes and explains the moral dilemmas relevant to the given subject.

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: Fails to recognize any ethical concerns pertinent to the competition's rules.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: Either recognizes the ethical problems or provides a definition for them, but not both.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: Successfully recognizes and explains the ethical problems.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: Successfully recognizes and explains the ethical problems, incorporating specialized vocabulary from the relevant field.

criteria.name: Explains why the ethical issues happened
criteria.description: Provides an account of the contributing factors that led to the emergence of the ethical problems.

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: Does not offer any explanations for the occurrence of the ethical problems.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: Identifies causes for the ethical problems, but these explanations are not accurate or relevant.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: Pinpoints multiple, though not every single, accurate cause for the ethical problems.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: Comprehensively covers and examines every underlying cause of the ethical problems.

criteria.name: Provides logical solutions as to how the ethical issues should be resolved
criteria.description: Offers sensible approaches for addressing and resolving the identified ethical dilemmas.

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: Fails to propose any remedies, or the suggested remedies bear no connection to the ethical problem identified.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: Presents a single resolution, but it is unclear, not pertinent, or not supported by sound reasoning.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: Offers one or two reasonable resolutions, demonstrating comprehension of the ethical problem and backed by some justification.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: Presents two or more thoroughly justified and plainly articulated resolutions, showcasing a deep grasp of the ethical problem and supported by reliable evidence or examination.

criteria.name: Recommends safeguards that should have been in place to prevent the ethical issues
criteria.description: Suggests protective measures that ought to have been established to avert the ethical problems.

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: No preventative measures are identified or put forward.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: Mentions one general preventative measure, but it lacks specific details, relevance, or an explanation of its effectiveness in preventing the issue.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: Proposes one or two pertinent preventative measures, along with a basic explanation of how they could have stopped the ethical issue.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: Recommends two or more clearly defined, practical preventative measures and explains them thoroughly, demonstrating a strong understanding of how each could have proactively dealt with or avoided the ethical challenge.

criteria.name: Substantiates and cites sources used while conducting research
criteria.description: Verifies information and references the sources utilized during the research process.

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: No sources are referenced.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: Sources or references are infrequently provided to back up claims.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: Generally includes credible and relevant sources and materials to support statements.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: Provides convincing evidence from credible and relevant sources and materials to support all statements.

criteria.name: Business Interviews & Integration of Insights
criteria.description: Incorporates insights gained from interviews with business professionals.

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: No discussions with business professionals are included, or any mentioned interviews lack explanation or relevance to the proposed recommendations.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: Fewer than three business professionals are interviewed, or their input is explained unclearly or only loosely connected to the recommendations.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: Three interviews are conducted, and the findings are summarized with a basic explanation of how these insights influenced the recommendations.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: Three or more interviews are clearly summarized, and the insights are thoughtfully analyzed and directly linked to the recommendations, demonstrating a strong integration of real-world perspectives.

criteria.name: Includes correct grammar, punctuation, and spelling
criteria.description: Ensures the use of proper grammar, punctuation, and spelling.

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: Contains five or more errors in grammar, punctuation, or spelling.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: Contains three to four errors in grammar, punctuation, or spelling.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: Contains no more than two errors in grammar, punctuation, or spelling.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: Contains no more than one error in grammar, punctuation, or spelling.

criteria.name: Adherence to Competitive Events Guidelines (Pre-Judged Asset)
criteria.description: The execution aligns with the guidelines: The Executive Summary was submitted as a PDF; no links or QR codes were used (unless in citations); the Executive Summary was no more than 3 pages (Page 1 for the title, Page 2 for the summary, Page 3 for works cited); and the Executive Summary was formatted for 8 1/2" x 11" paper.

levels.label: Execution Aligned with Guidelines: (All criteria must be met)

criteria.name: Objective Test
criteria.description: Each participant will complete a multiple-choice test consisting of 100 questions. The test items are based on the knowledge areas and objectives specified for this event: Communication Skills (10 questions), Self-Awareness (15 questions), Doing the Right Thing (30 questions), Teamwork Skills (15 questions), Leadership Skills (20 questions), and Career Readiness (10 questions).

criteria.name: Identifies and defines ethical issues presented in the topic
criteria.description: Clearly recognizes and explains the moral dilemmas relevant to the given subject.

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: Fails to recognize any ethical concerns pertinent to the competition's rules.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: Either recognizes the ethical problems or provides a definition for them, but not both.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: Successfully recognizes and explains the ethical problems.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: Successfully recognizes and explains the ethical problems, incorporating specialized vocabulary from the relevant field.

criteria.name: Explains why the ethical issues happened
criteria.description: Provides an account of the contributing factors that led to the emergence of the ethical problems.

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: Does not offer any explanations for the occurrence of the ethical problems.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: Identifies a single cause for the ethical issue(s), but it is not accurate or relevant.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: Identifies a single accurate and relevant cause for the ethical issue(s).

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: Identifies and analyzes two or more accurate and relevant causes.

criteria.name: Provides logical recommendations as to how the ethical issues should be resolved
criteria.description: Offers sensible approaches for addressing and resolving the identified ethical dilemmas.

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: Fails to propose any remedies, or the suggested remedies bear no connection to the ethical problem identified.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: Presents a single resolution, but it is unclear, not pertinent, or not supported by sound reasoning.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: Offers one or two reasonable resolutions, demonstrating comprehension of the ethical problem and backed by some justification.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: Presents two or more thoroughly justified and plainly articulated resolutions, showcasing a deep grasp of the ethical problem and supported by reliable evidence or examination.

criteria.name: Recommends safeguards that should have been in place to prevent the ethical issues
criteria.description: Suggests protective measures that ought to have been established to avert the ethical problems.

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: No preventative measures are identified or put forward.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: Mentions one general preventative measure, but it lacks specific details, relevance, or an explanation of its effectiveness in preventing the issue.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: Proposes one or two pertinent preventative measures, along with a basic explanation of how they could have stopped the ethical issue.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: Recommends two or more clearly defined, practical preventative measures and explains them thoroughly, demonstrating a strong understanding of how each could have proactively dealt with or avoided the ethical challenge.

criteria.name: Research shows quality and related information to the ethical issues and incorporates input of businesspeople interviewed
criteria.description: The research demonstrates high quality and relevance to the ethical issues, incorporating input from interviewed business professionals.

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: No research was conducted, and the presentation contains three or more inaccurate statements.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: The research is not relevant to the ethical topic, and the presentation includes one to two inaccurate statements.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: All research presented is accurate, but no supporting evidence is referenced.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: The research is accurate with supporting evidence provided, and it integrates insights from interviewed business professionals as part of the presentation.

criteria.name: Substantiates and cites sources used while conducting research
criteria.description: Verifies information and references the sources utilized during the research process.

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: No sources are referenced.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: Sources or references are infrequently provided to back up claims.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: Generally includes credible and relevant sources and materials to support statements.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: Provides convincing evidence from credible and relevant sources and materials to support all statements.

criteria.name: Statements are well-organized and clearly stated
criteria.description: The information presented is structured logically and articulated with clarity.

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: The competitor(s) did not appear to be prepared.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: The competitor(s) were prepared, but the presentation lacked a logical flow.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: The presentation progressed in a logical order.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: The presentation progressed in a logical order, and the statements were well-structured.

criteria.name: Consistently displays confidence, poised body language, engaging eye contact, and effective voice projection.
criteria.description: Consistently exhibits self-assurance, composed body language, engaging eye contact, and effective voice projection.

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: Did not exhibit any of the specified communication skills.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: Exhibited one or two of the specified communication skills (confidence, body language, eye contact, or voice projection).

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: Exhibited three of the specified communication skills (confidence, body language, eye contact, or voice projection).

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: Exhibited all specified communication skills, thereby enhancing the overall presentation.

criteria.name: Demonstrates the ability to effectively answer questions
criteria.description: Shows proficiency in providing effective answers to inquiries.

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: Does not respond to questions, or responses are entirely irrelevant.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: Provides incomplete or unclear answers, indicating a limited grasp of the subject.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: Responds accurately and clearly to most questions, demonstrating adequate understanding.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: Responds with confidence, clarity, accuracy, and thoughtful answers that enhance the overall presentation.

criteria.name: Adherence to Competitive Events Guidelines (Presentation)
criteria.description: The execution aligns with the guidelines: only permissible technology devices were used (sizing specifications were followed; a maximum of two, with only one facing judges at a time); the presentation was relevant to the assigned topic; professional boundaries were maintained during set-up time (no interaction with judges); no materials were left behind after the presentation; links or QR codes were displayed appropriately (not clicked or scanned by judges); audio was presented without external speakers (preliminary round); and the use of food or live animals was avoided.

levels.label: Execution Aligned with Guidelines: (All criteria must be met)
